title: dryopteris crinipes
intro: Dryopteris crinipes, also known as the Hair-stalked Wood fern, is a species of fern native to the Himalayas. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Dryopteris crinipes include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a powder, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects.
